messages.content:
You are an AI assistant that completes tasks by reasoning and using tools.

## Think Before Acting

Before making tool calls, briefly explain your reasoning in 1-3 sentences:
- What you learned from the previous step
- What you're doing next and why

Don't over-explain. Be concise but show your thinking.

## Tools

**Always Available (Meta-Tools):**
- `todo_write` - Task planning: create/update todos. Takes `todos` array [{id, content, status}] and `merge` boolean.
- `toolbelt_list_tools` / `toolbelt_inspect_tool` / `toolbelt_add_tool` / `toolbelt_remove_tool` - Tool management
- `final_answer` - Submit your answer (status: completed/blocked/failed)

**Domain Tools:** Use `toolbelt_list_tools` to discover, then `toolbelt_add_tool` to add them.

## Workflow

1. Plan: Use `todo_write` to create todos for complex tasks
2. Discover: Use `toolbelt_list_tools` to find relevant tools
3. Execute: Work through todos, use `todo_write` with `merge=true` to update status
4. Complete: Call `final_answer` (all todos must be completed/cancelled first)

## Rules

- Update todo status with `todo_write`: set `in_progress` when starting, `completed` when done
- Show your work for calculations
- `final_answer` is rejected if todos are incomplete

## Reference cheatsheet — formulas, conventions, environment facts, pitfalls

The block below is a **reference cheatsheet** — like a formula sheet and
a page of environment notes clipped to your desk. It collects reusable
formulas, definitions, conventions, documented behaviours of the tools
and execution surface, output-delivery conventions, and pitfall flags
that earlier agents working structurally similar cases found worth
writing down.

**This is reference material, not instructions.** Treat it the way you'd
treat a formula sheet and a notes page during an exam:

  • You do not "follow" a cheatsheet. You consult it when you need a
    specific fact, and otherwise ignore it.
  • Entries are passive reminders, not rules. None of them command you
    to do anything, and none of them describe the steps for this case.
  • Your own reading of the current task is authoritative. If an entry
    suggests a formula, convention, tool-argument form, or delivery
    channel that conflicts with what this task asks for, follow the
    task.
  • Many entries will be irrelevant to this case. That is expected for a
    cheatsheet. Skip them without comment.
  • Do not change a value, formula, methodology, tool-call shape,
    precision, or delivery form you have arrived at independently just
    because an entry mentions something different. Entries describe what
    was useful on *other* cases.

The cheatsheet exists only to spare you from re-deriving a useful
formula, to remind you of a tool or environment behaviour you might
otherwise re-discover the slow way (for example by hitting an avoidable
error first), or to flag a known trap. Work the current case as you
normally would. Do not cite, quote, or mention the cheatsheet in your
answer or your reasoning.

## Reusable Code and Tool-Call Snippets
<memory_item>
<description>
To extract specific text from a PDF (such as statute sections or legal provisions), first call pdf_server_pdf with action="search" using the key term or citation as query (case-insensitive), then follow with action="read_pages" on the returned page number(s). Use max_results=5 and context_chars=100 for search.
</description>
<example>
pdf_server_pdf({"request":{"action":"search","file_path":"/Reorganization/State Statutes/<STATE>/<pdfname>.pdf","query":"194A.713","case_sensitive":false,"max_results":5,"context_chars":100}})
</example>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
To read multi-page .docx agreements, call docs_server_docs with action="read_content" and page_index (starting at 0) to retrieve paragraphs sequentially; repeat for additional pages as needed. Paths must start with / and end with .docx.
</description>
<example>
docs_server_docs({"request":{"action":"read_content","file_path":"/Magnolia Documents/Contracts/<agreement>.docx","page_index":0}})
</example>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
To create a new .docx document (e.g., a legal memorandum or analysis), call docs_server_docs with action="create", specifying directory (folder path) and file_name (ending with .docx) separately, along with content as an array of valid content blocks (each object must have a "type" field such as "heading" or "paragraph" with required fields populated; empty objects or incorrect parameter names like file_path will fail validation).
</description>
<example>
docs_server_docs({"request":{"action":"create","directory":"/Litigation Strategy/Wrongful Death Documents/<case_name>","file_name":"Memo.docx","content":[{"type":"heading","text":"Title","level":1},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Body text here."}]}})
</example>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
To compute dates, day of week, or time intervals in the sandbox when direct Python execution fails, use code_execution_server_code_exec with a shell date command (e.g., date -d 'YYYY-MM-DD' +%A or similar formatting).
</description>
<example>
code_execution_server_code_exec({"request":{"code":"date -d '2025-08-06' +%A"}})
</example>
</memory_item>

## Solution Strategies for Recurring Task Shapes
<memory_item>
<description>
For compliance reviews of assisted living residency agreements against state statutes, first locate and extract the exact statutory requirement(s) from the state PDF, then read the target agreement document(s) page-by-page, and compare each enumerated statutory element against the agreement language for presence or omission. When multiple similar agreement files exist, select the most general or state-matched version rather than a state-specific variant.
</description>
<example>
Locate statute PDF → search/read target section → read agreement → map each statutory item (e.g., notice periods, fee provisions) to agreement text.
</example>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
For evaluating a motion for summary judgment on a wrongful death claim including a crumbling skull rule affirmative defense, locate the complaint, moving party's expert report or affidavit (e.g., on cause of death), and opposing evidence such as a deposition; assess admissibility of the expert opinions (qualified expert, reliable basis in case-file facts, not speculation) and whether any evidence raises a genuine issue of material fact on whether the negligence accelerated the pre-existing deterioration. If factual disputes exist on key elements or the defense, the motion is likely denied.
</description>
<example>
Locate complaint and Expert Witness Opinion Letter.docx in litigation folder → read deposition of key witness (e.g., on care protocols) → compare evidence for triable issues on duty, breach, causation, and crumbling skull acceleration under civil procedure rules.
</summary>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
For compliance reviews against specific regulatory sections (e.g., NY Part 487 on meals/snacks, TX Chapter 276 on deposits, KY 194A.713 on notice, or CA CCR Title 22 §87507), locate the relevant PDF in /Reorganization/State Statutes/<STATE>/ or root-level rules PDFs, use pdf_server_pdf search for key terms (multiple targeted searches if needed), read_pages on matches, and compare provided operational facts against the exact rule language.
</description>
<example>
Locate state PDF → search for regulation keywords (e.g., "meals", "snacks", "pet deposit", "service animal", "87507") → read relevant pages → check if facts satisfy the rule.
</summary>
</memory_item>

## Formulas, Definitions, and Conventions
<memory_item>
<description>
KRS 194A.713(7) and analogous state provisions require a minimum 30-day notice for fee structure changes in assisted living lease agreements; absence of any such notice language constitutes non-compliance.
</description>
<example>
Statutory element (7): "Minimum thirty (30) day notice provision for a change in the community's fee structure."
</summary>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
KRS 194A.713(14) requires that an assisted living lease agreement include grievance policies that minimally address issues related to confidentiality of complaints and the process for resolving grievances between the resident and the assisted living community; absence of such policies constitutes non-compliance.
</description>
<example>
Statutory element (14): "Grievance policies that minimally address issues related to confidentiality of complaints and the process for resolving grievances between the resident and the assisted living community."
</summary>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
The crumbling skull rule limits a defendant's tort liability to damages caused by accelerating or worsening a pre-existing condition that was independently deteriorating; the defendant is not responsible for the natural progression of the condition. This contrasts with the eggshell skull rule, which holds the defendant liable for all consequences regardless of the plaintiff's pre-existing susceptibility. In New York wrongful death cases, an admissible expert report on cause of death (qualified expert, grounded in case facts, non-speculative) can support a crumbling skull affirmative defense on summary judgment.
</description>
<example>
Crumbing skull analysis: if expert report demonstrates death would have occurred from independent progression of brittle diabetes and comorbidities, liability limited to any additional harm from negligence.
</summary>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
KRS 194A.713(8) requires that an assisted living lease agreement include a minimum thirty (30) day move-out notice provision for resident nonpayment, subject to applicable landlord or tenant laws; absence of such language constitutes non-compliance.
</description>
<example>
Statutory element (8): "Minimum thirty (30) day move-out notice provision for resident nonpayment, subject to applicable landlord or tenant laws."
</summary>
</memory_item>

## Environment and Sandbox Facts
<memory_item>
<description>
State statutes for assisted living compliance are stored under /Reorganization/State Statutes/<STATE ABBREV>/ as PDFs (e.g., 194A_700-7205.pdf for Kentucky). Use filesystem_server_list_files or get_directory_tree on /Reorganization/State Statutes/ to discover available states.
</description>
<example>
/Reorganization/State Statutes/KY/ contains 194A_700-7205.pdf and Regs 902 KAR 20 480.pdf
</summary>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
Magnolia-specific agreements reside in /Magnolia Documents/Contracts/; files include General Resident Agreement Template.docx and state-variant versions (e.g., Gardens Residency Agreement Martin Miller v1.docx for California).
</description>
<example>
filesystem_server_list_files({"path":"/Magnolia Documents/Contracts"})
</summary>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
Litigation-related documents, including complaints, strategies, and case files for wrongful death, breach of contract, and assisted living matters, are stored under /Litigation Strategy/ with subfolders such as Assisted Living Litigation in General/ (containing PDFs), Breach of Contract Documents/ (containing complaints and related files), and Wrongful Death Documents/. Use filesystem_server_list_files or get_directory_tree on /Litigation Strategy/ to discover available files.
</description>
<example>
/Litigation Strategy/ contains Assisted Living Litigation in General/, Breach of Contract Documents/, and Wrongful Death Documents/
</summary>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
Breach of contract complaints and related filings are stored under /Litigation Strategy/Breach of Contract Documents/ with possible subfolders such as Complaints/. Use filesystem_server_list_files on that path to locate original, first amended, and second amended versions.
</description>
<example>
filesystem_server_list_files({"path":"/Litigation Strategy/Breach of Contract Documents"})
</summary>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
Case-specific wrongful death files (complaints, incident reports, depositions, affidavits, medical logs) are located in subfolders under /Litigation Strategy/Wrongful Death Documents/<resident or case name e.g. Tom Buchanan>/. List files there to find documents like Wrongful Death Complaint.docx or deposition transcripts.
</description>
<example>
filesystem_server_list_files({"path":"/Litigation Strategy/Wrongful Death Documents/Tom Buchanan"})
</summary>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
New York regulations (e.g., NYCRR Part 487 or Title 18 sections on assisted living) are stored under /Reorganization/State Statutes/NY/ as PDFs. Use filesystem_server_list_files on /Reorganization/State Statutes/NY/ or root-level rules PDFs to locate files covering meals, snacks, or operational requirements.
</description>
<example>
/Reorganization/State Statutes/NY/ contains relevant PDFs for NYCRR compliance checks.
</summary>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
California regulations (e.g., CCR Title 22 sections on residential care facilities for the elderly) are stored under /Reorganization/State Statutes/CA/ as PDFs. Use filesystem_server_list_files on /Reorganization/State Statutes/CA/ to locate files covering resident agreements or operational requirements.
</description>
<example>
/Reorganization/State Statutes/CA/ contains relevant PDFs for CCR Title 22 compliance checks.
</summary>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
North Carolina regulations for Adult Care Homes (e.g., 10A NCAC 13F) are stored under /Reorganization/State Statutes/NC/ as PDFs. Use filesystem_server_list_files on /Reorganization/State Statutes/NC/ to locate files for compliance checks on facility features.
</description>
<example>
/Reorganization/State Statutes/NC/ contains relevant PDFs for 10A NCAC compliance checks.
</summary>
</memory_item>

## Pitfalls, Edge Cases, and Verification Checks
<memory_item>
<description>
When multiple residency agreement files exist, verify the selected file matches the task scope (general template vs. state-specific or resident-specific version) before comparison; California RCFE forms may contain 60-day notice language that satisfies a 30-day minimum but is irrelevant to other states.
</description>
<example>
Confirm agreement file name and content scope before mapping to statute (e.g., avoid using CA-specific form for KY compliance check).
</summary>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
When creating new .docx files with docs_server_docs action="create", the content parameter must be a list of valid block objects (each with a "type" field such as "heading" or "paragraph"); passing empty objects, empty lists, or structures missing the type tag causes repeated validation errors. Always specify directory and file_name separately.
</description>
<example>
Use content=[{"type":"heading","text":"Title","level":1},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Body text."}] ; do not pass {} or invalid tags.
</summary>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
For tasks involving identification of objectionable testimony in deposition transcripts under a cited evidence rule (such as NY Rule of Evidence 10.03), locate and read the deposition file (e.g., Nurse Deposition.docx) using sequential read_content calls to access the specific paragraph referenced in the task, then analyze statements for violations like lack of personal knowledge, speculation, or hearsay.
</description>
<example>
Use docs_server_docs to read Nurse Deposition.docx pages until paragraph 8 is located; flag statements in that paragraph that fail the rule's requirements for admissibility.
</summary>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
When checking compliance with a specific regulation (e.g., NY Part 487 on meal planning or similar), confirm the exact statutory text and requirements match the scenario facts (such as advance planning interval or snack frequency) before concluding compliance or non-compliance; search multiple related terms if initial results are narrow.
</description>
<example>
Confirm the regulatory text on "meals" or "snacks" directly addresses the provided operational detail (e.g., planning two and a half weeks in advance) before using it.
</summary>
</memory_item>

---
We are conducting a compliance review of Magnolia Gardens' General Resident Agreement. We are currently determining whether the General Resident Agreement is in compliance with Kentucky Law. Please review the General Resident Agreement and determine if it is in compliance with Kentucky Revised Statutes (KRS) section 194A.713(8). Please provide a short paragraph in reply to me right here, explaining your reasoning.

messages.reasoning_content:

First, the task is to review if the General Resident Agreement is in compliance with KRS 194A.713(8), and provide a short paragraph explaining reasoning.

From cheatsheet: KRS 194A.

{"action":"read_content","error":null,"help":null,"create":null,"delete":null,"overview":null,"read_content":{"raw_output":"Document Content: /Magnolia Documents/Contracts/General Resident Agreement Template.docx
================================================================================

Status: success
Paragraphs in response: 50
Tables: 0
Images: 0

PAGINATION: Total 2 pages (use page_index 0 to 1)
  Current page: 0 (paragraphs 0 to 49)

================================================================================

Paragraphs:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
[body.p.0]: ASSISTED LIVING RESIDENT AGREEMENT 
  Runs: [body.p.0.r.0]: "ASSISTED LIVING RESIDENT AGREEMENT"(bold,underline) | [body.p.0.r.1]: " "(bold)

[body.p.1]: You are strongly encourage to have your attorney or other representative review this agreement before  you sign. ____________________ is permitted by zoning and the fire Marshal to have ___ total  residents; we are licensed to have _____ Assisted Living residents. This month to month lease is for  Assisted Living residents only. All independent living residents shall not be a part of the assisted living  program and will not receive care by assisted living program aids and employees. Per state  requirements all Independent living residents shall be assessed by the delegating nurse to assure that  an assisted living level of care is not needed and that the resident is qualified for Independent living;  this assessment will cost $____.  
  Runs: [body.p.1.r.0]: "You are strongly "(italic) | [body.p.1.r.1]: "encourage"(italic) | [body.p.1.r.2]: " to have your attorney or other representative review this agreement before  you "(italic) | [body.p.1.r.3]: "sign"(italic) | [body.p.1.r.4]: ". ____________________ is permitted by zoning and the fire Marshal to have ___ total  residents; we are licensed to "(italic) | [body.p.1.r.5]: "have __"(italic) | [body.p.1.r.6]: "___ Assisted Living residents. This month to month lease is for  Assisted Living residents only. All independent living residents shall "(italic) | [body.p.1.r.7]: "n"(bold,italic) | [body.p.1.r.8]: "ot "(bold,italic,underline) | [body.p.1.r.9]: "be a part of the assisted living  program and will not receive care "(italic) | [body.p.1.r.10]: "by"(italic) | [body.p.1.r.11]: " assisted living program aids and employees. Per state  requirements all "(italic) | [body.p.1.r.12]: "Independent"(italic) | [body.p.1.r.13]: " living residents shall be assessed by the delegating nurse to assure that  an assisted living level of care is not needed and that the resident is qualified for "(italic) | [body.p.1.r.14]: "Independent"(italic) | [body.p.1.r.15]: " living;  this assessment will cost $____.  "(italic)

[body.p.2]: PARTIES 
  Runs: [body.p.2.r.0]: "PARTIES "(bold)

[body.p.3]: 1. This agreement is between (company)__________________________ and________________________________________ (Residents Name) 

[body.p.4]: Your residency at _______________________________ begins  

[body.p.5]: on____________________. After this agreement is signed this agreement will at such  time become valid and in effect and with the understanding that the charges of all  required fees for the current month is to be collected by ____________________. 

[body.p.6]: LEVEL OF CARE 
  Runs: [body.p.6.r.0]: "LEVEL OF CARE "(bold)

[body.p.7]: 2. This facility is licensed to provide low, moderate, and high levels of care. 

[body.p.8]: 3.Based on the information provided by your doctor and an assessment performed by  this facility, you require ______ level of care. if your care needs change and you need a  higher level of care, which this facility is not licensed to provide, we may request a level  of care waiver from the Department in order for you to remain here. if the waiver  request is not granted, we will give you ample time notice that you will be discharged,  and will assist you in finding an appropriate facility. 

[body.p.9]: FEES 
  Runs: [body.p.9.r.0]: "FEES "(bold)

[body.p.10]: 4.The monthly fee for your care at this facility is $_______________ with an entry fee  of $________________. This monthly fee includes the services listed below. This fee  does not include medications, personal phone line/long distance calls, medical  equipment, outside entertainment or trips, purchasing of clothing, adult day care,  barbering, hair styling, manicuring, burial and funeral expenses and arrangements.

[body.p.11]:  List your ALF on ssMaps.com and get clients 
  Runs: [body.p.11.r.1]: "List your ALF on ssMaps.com and get clients "(italic)

[body.p.12]: Assisted Living facility that are participating in the older Adult waver may not  charge waver participants any additional fees such as entry fees, late payment fees,  etc. We do not charge Waiver participants any additional fees. Such as entry fees,  late payment fees etc.  
  Runs: [body.p.12.r.0]: "Assisted Living facility that are participating in the older "(bold) | [body.p.12.r.1]: "Adult"(bold) | [body.p.12.r.2]: " waver may not  charge waver participants any additional fees such as entry fees, late payment fees,  etc. We do not charge Waiver participants any additional fees. Such as entry fees,  late payment fees etc.  "(bold)

[body.p.13]: • Medicaid Subsidy: ____________________________ 

[body.p.14]: • State Subsidy: ________________________________ 

[body.p.15]: • Facility Subsidy: ______________________________ 

[body.p.16]: • TOTAL FEES: ________________________________ 

[body.p.17]: SERVICES 
  Runs: [body.p.17.r.0]: "SERVICES "(bold)

[body.p.18]: 5. In consideration of your monthly payment, the facility agrees to provide the following  services: 

[body.p.19]: a. A private/semi private room which includes a bed, bedside table lamp, chair, dresser;  bath linens and bed linens; 

[body.p.20]: b. Meals which include three meals a days and additional snacks 

[body.p.21]: c. Personal care services which include assistance with eating, personal hygiene,  transferring, toileting„ and dressing. 

[body.p.22]: d. Laundry and housekeeping services, 

[body.p.23]: e. Assistance with access to health care, social services, and social activities. f. Reminders or physical assistance to residents who can self-administer  medications/administration of medication. 

[body.p.24]: g. basic hygiene, grooming, bathing and oral hygiene, shaving equipment, lotions,  powders, deodorants, shampoo, comb/brush etc. 

[body.p.25]: OCCUPANCY PROVISIONS 
  Runs: [body.p.25.r.0]: "OCCUPANCY PROVISIONS "(bold)

[body.p.26]: . 

[body.p.27]: 6.You are assigned to bedroom #____________ and bed #_______________ 

[body.p.28]: 7.If it becomes necessary because of health, safety or other conditions to move your  bedroom or bed assignment, the facility will give you at least 5 days advanced notice. 

[body.p.29]: 8.If your care needs become greater than the facility can safely handle, it may become  necessary to transfer you to another facility. In that event, you will be given at least 30  days notice before the transfer and assistance with transmitting to your new home. 

[body.p.30]: 9.Locks are available for your use in securing personal belonging. 

[body.p.31]: 10.This facility follows security provisions to ensure your safety and well-being:

[body.p.32]:  List your ALF on ssMaps.com and get clients 
  Runs: [body.p.32.r.1]: "List your ALF on ssMaps.com and get clients "(italic)

[body.p.33]: • Alarmed entry and exits 

[body.p.34]: • Requirement to notify staff when leaving facility and length of absence • No locks on residents' room door. Door locks(inside and out) are required on all  residents rooms controlled by resident with staff access by permission or in case  of emergency.  

[body.p.35]: • Log for residents, staff and visitors to sign in/out facility. 

[body.p.36]: 11. Residents have full use of their own rooms and the common areas of the facility. 

[body.p.37]: 12. To ensure your safety and wellbeing, the staff has the right to enter your room;  however, the staff 

[body.p.38]: will make every effort to be respectful of your privacy and will always knock before  entering. 

[body.p.39]: 13. In the event you are on leave of absence from the facility for hospitalization,  vacation or other reason, the facility will hold your bed provided you are able to provide  payment for the duration of leave and the facility is eligible to meet the care needs of the  resident. The Medicaid waver program does not pay for bed hold services, that is  the sole responsibility of the resident. 
  Runs: [body.p.39.r.5]: "The Medicaid waver program does not pay for bed "(bold) | [body.p.39.r.6]: "hold services, that is  the sole responsibility of the resident. "(bold)

[body.p.40]: 14. In the event of an emergency situation which could make it unsafe or unhealthy to  continue to provide services at the facility, the facility will make arrangements to  temporarily relocate you to another location as directed by the Director of the facility, all  residents will be relocated to_________________________________________. 

[body.p.41]: 15. The resident rules of the facility are attached to this agreement and incorporated by  reference. By signing this agreement, you have indicated acknowledgement and receipt  of the resident rules and 

[body.p.42]: agree to abide by these rules. 

[body.p.43]: 16. The following special information is required in the untimely event of death: 

[body.p.44]: Burial Arrangements 
  Runs: [body.p.44.r.0]: "Burial Arrangements "(bold)

[body.p.45]: Financial____________________________________________________ Religious____________________________________________________ Person Who has burial responsibility: 

[body.p.46]: Name:____________________________ Relationship_________________ Address_______________________________________________________ ________________________________________________________

[body.p.47]:  List your ALF on ssMaps.com and get clients 
  Runs: [body.p.47.r.1]: "List your ALF on ssMaps.com and get clients "(italic)

[body.p.48]: Phone_________________________________________________________ 

[body.p.49]: ADMISSION & DISCHARGE POLICIES 
  Runs: [body.p.49.r.0]: "ADMISSION & DISCHARGE POLICIES "(bold)
"},"read_image":null,"add_text":null,"edit_text":null,"delete_text":null,"add_image":null,"modify_image":null,"format":null,"header_footer":null,"page_margins":null,"page_orientation":null,"comments":null}

[body.p.50]: 17. You may be discharged from the facility for the following reasons: • Non-payment of fees 

[body.p.51]: • Care needs exceed what the facility is able to provide 

[body.p.52]: • c Behavioral reasons, including but not limited to destruction of property,  violence, sexual misconduct, and not abiding by house rules. 

[body.p.53]: 18. in the event the facility decides to discharge you, you will be given at least 30 days  advanced notice prior to the date of discharge in the event you are discharged because of  health emergency, the facility may not be able to give you 30 days notice. 

[body.p.54]: 19. If you wish to leave the facility you are required to give 30day notice of date you  wish to terminate this agreement: However if you are leaving because of health  emergency 30day notice is not required. 

[body.p.55]: COMPLAINT AND GRIEVANCE PROCEDURES 
  Runs: [body.p.55.r.0]: "COMPLAINT AND GRIEVANCE PROCEDURES "(bold)

[body.p.56]: 20.A copy of resident's rights is attached and incorporated by reference into this  agreement. This facility will honor and respect your rights. 

[body.p.57]: 21.You have the right to make suggestions, register complaints or present grievances  about the care of service you or another resident receives here. You may address these  concerns ___________________. (____-_____-_______) Assisted living program  manager, or you may contact Assisted Living Complaint Unit at _____-___-____or tool  free at _-___-___-_______. 

[body.p.58]: 22.If your Complaint is directed to ALH manager, you will receive a response to your  complaint within 5 days If you are not satisfied with that response or the ALH manager  does not respond to your complaint, you may contact the Assisted Living Complaint  Unit. at ________________________ . 

[body.p.59]: 23.The facility is responsible for monitoring and recording residents health status. 24.The facility will not handle residents finances if resident is unable. 
  Runs: [body.p.59.r.1]: "not "(bold)

[body.p.60]: 25.The facility is responsible for arranging for or overseeing your care and for  contracting for services including equipment and supplies not provided by the facility. It 

[body.p.61]:  List your ALF on ssMaps.com and get clients 
  Runs: [body.p.61.r.1]: "List your ALF on ssMaps.com and get clients "(italic)

[body.p.62]: is the facilities responsibility to oversee the residents care and contract for services not  supplied by the facility. Case manager will assist waver participant to procure needs  medical supplies and medical equipment as authorized in their plan of care waver  participants may identify the assistive devices and equipment waver provider of their  choice through their case manager. 

[body.p.63]: 26.If for any reason you have not taken your personal property with you upon discharge,  the facility will pack up your belongings and safely store them for 20 days. If you or  your family have not retrieved them within 20 days of discharge, your property will be  disposed of. 

[body.p.64]: 27.Residents are not mandated to attend adult daycare. He or she has the right to choose  weather they want to attend. The facility will comply with the residents' plan of care  regarding the number the number per week of the attendance in the day care program. 

[body.p.65]: IN WHITNESS WHEREOF, THE PARTIES HAVE EXICUTED THIS  AGREEMENT 
  Runs: [body.p.65.r.0]: "IN WHITNESS WHEREOF, THE PARTIES HAVE EXICUTED THIS  AGREEMENT "(bold)

[body.p.66]: ON THIS_________ DAY OF_________ YEAR__________________ 

[body.p.67]: Whitness:___________________________ Facility:  

[body.p.68]: by:________________________________ 

[body.p.69]:  Signature 

[body.p.70]: Name:_____________________________ 

[body.p.71]:  Print 

[body.p.72]: Title:_______________________________ 

[body.p.73]: RESIDENT OR RESPONSIBLE PARTY 
  Runs: [body.p.73.r.0]: "RESIDENT OR RESPONSIBLE PARTY "(italic)

[body.p.74]: _________________________________________ 
  Runs: [body.p.74.r.0]: "_________________________________________ "(italic)

[body.p.75]: Signature 
  Runs: [body.p.75.r.0]: "Signature "(italic)

[body.p.76]: _________________________________________ 
  Runs: [body.p.76.r.0]: "_________________________________________ "(italic)

[body.p.77]: Date

messages.reasoning_content:
Now I have the full content. Key parts for discharge/nonpayment:
First, the task is: review the General Resident Agreement for compliance with KRS 194A.713(8). Provide a short paragraph explaining reasoning, and report confidence inside \confidence{}.

From the cheatsheet:

KRS 194A.713(8) requires that an assisted living lease agreement include a minimum 30 (30) day move-out notice provision for resident nonpayment, subject to applicable landlord or tenant laws; absence of such language constitutes non-compliance.

Example: Statutory element (8): "Minimum thirty (30) day move-out notice provision for resident nonpayment, subject to applicable landlord or tenant laws.

messages.content: The General Resident Agreement Template complies with KRS 194A.713(8). The statute requires inclusion of a minimum thirty (30) day move-out notice provision for resident nonpayment. Section 17 of the agreement lists non-payment of fees as a discharge reason, and Section 18 mandates at least 30 days advanced notice prior to any discharge (except in health emergencies), directly satisfying the statutory notice requirement for nonpayment scenarios.
